prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `f8cc533f`.
codex_response:
Implemented `task_f8cc533f` in [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_f8cc533f/__init__.py), [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_f8cc533f/verifier.py), [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_f8cc533f/generator.py), and [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_f8cc533f/notes.md). The rule is: each colored object is a damaged copy of one shared square template; normalize all foreground objects to their own upper-left corners, union those normalized shapes to recover the template, then repaint that template at every object's original upper-left corner in the object's color.

I discarded both summary hints. The Sonnet hint was wrong about vertical-only symmetry, and the Opus hint was still misleading because the task is not solved independently per object; the key is the cross-object normalized union. The generator matches the family by creating one symmetric square template, producing several damaged color-specific copies of it, and placing them on a uniform background with spacing similar to the official task. No new primitive was needed.

Validation: `python -m arc2.shared.verify --task f8cc533f` passed `5/5` official examples. `python -m arc2.shared.build --task f8cc533f --n-examples 1000` completed successfully, and I visually checked [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/f8cc533f/previews/preview_01.png) and [preview_02.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/f8cc533f/previews/preview_02.png).
